brief_notes: Ambitious multi-omic atlas study of immune aging across 220 individuals and 13 age groups using 5 technologies (scRNA-seq, TCR/BCR-seq, CyTOF, bulk RNA-seq, flow cytometry). Key findings are compelling: GNLY+CD8+ EM T cell clonal expansion, novel cytotoxic B cells in children, and immune aging clock (r=0.92, MAE 5.8 years). Weaknesses: methods section lists only 10 of 13 age groups, CyTOF and bulk RNA-seq results are not shown in any table, the cytotoxic B cell finding needs functional validation beyond expression data, the aging clock uses LOO-CV which can overfit with 220 samples (k-fold or held-out test would be stronger), limited references (~10), and single-cohort Chinese population limits generalizability as acknowledged. The paper attempts to cover too much ground, resulting in some findings being underdeveloped.
